channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Odd-Chern-Number Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect at Even Filling in Moire Rhombohedral Heptalayer Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Qianling Liu, Zhiyu Wang, Xiangyan Han, Zhuoxian Li, Bohao Li, Sicheng Zhou, Lihong Hu, Zhuangzhuang Qu, Chunrui Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Zheng Vitto Han, Bingbing Tong, Guangtong Liu, Li Lu, Fengcheng Wu, and Jianming Lu

The quantum anomalous Hall effect at even filling has been observed in rhombohedral heptalayer graphene moiré superlattices – previous observations were for odd-integer filling factors.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016602] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qianling Liu, Zhiyu Wang, Xiangyan Han, Zhuoxian Li, Bohao Li, Sicheng Zhou, Lihong Hu, Zhuangzhuang Qu, Chunrui Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Zheng Vitto Han, Bingbing Tong, Guangtong Liu, Li Lu, Fengcheng Wu, and Jianming Lu

The quantum anomalous Hall effect at even filling has been observed in rhombohedral heptalayer graphene moiré superlattices – previous observations were for odd-integer filling factors.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016602] Published Mon Jan 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Odd-Chern-Number Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect at Even Filling in Moire Rhombohedral Heptalayer Graphene
item.dc:creator: Qianling Liu, Zhiyu Wang, Xiangyan Han, Zhuoxian Li, Bohao Li, Sicheng Zhou, Lihong Hu, Zhuangzhuang Qu, Chunrui Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Zheng Vitto Han, Bingbing Tong, Guangtong Liu, Li Lu, Fengcheng Wu, and Jianming Lu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Common Sublattice-Pure Van Hove Singularities in the Kagome Superconductors $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A=\mathrm{K}$, Rb, Cs)
item.description:
Author(s): Yujie Lan, Yuhao Lei, Congcong Le, Brenden R. Ortiz, Nicholas C. Plumb, Milan Radovic, Xianxin Wu, Ming Shi, Stephen D. Wilson, and Yong Hu

Kagome materials offer a versatile platform for exploring correlated and topological quantum states, where Van Hove singularities (VHSs) play a pivotal role in driving electronic instabilities, exhibiting distinct behaviors depending on electron filling and interaction settings. In the recently disc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016401] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yujie Lan, Yuhao Lei, Congcong Le, Brenden R. Ortiz, Nicholas C. Plumb, Milan Radovic, Xianxin Wu, Ming Shi, Stephen D. Wilson, and Yong Hu

Kagome materials offer a versatile platform for exploring correlated and topological quantum states, where Van Hove singularities (VHSs) play a pivotal role in driving electronic instabilities, exhibiting distinct behaviors depending on electron filling and interaction settings. In the recently disc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 016401] Published Fri Jan 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Common Sublattice-Pure Van Hove Singularities in the Kagome Superconductors $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A=\mathrm{K}$, Rb, Cs)
item.dc:creator: Yujie Lan, Yuhao Lei, Congcong Le, Brenden R. Ortiz, Nicholas C. Plumb, Milan Radovic, Xianxin Wu, Ming Shi, Stephen D. Wilson, and Yong Hu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Chiral Superconductivity from Spin Polarized Chern Band in Twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Cheng Xu, Nianlong Zou, Nikolai Peshcherenko, Ammar Jahin, Tingxin Li, Shi-Zeng Lin, and Yang Zhang

Superconductivity has been observed in twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$ within the anomalous Hall metal parent state [Xu et al., arXiv:2504.06972.]. Key signatures—including a fully spin and valley-polarized normal state, anomalous Hall resistivity hysteresis, superconducting phase adjacent to the frac…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266005] Published Tue Dec 30, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Cheng Xu, Nianlong Zou, Nikolai Peshcherenko, Ammar Jahin, Tingxin Li, Shi-Zeng Lin, and Yang Zhang

Superconductivity has been observed in twisted MoTe2 within the anomalous Hall metal parent state [Xu et al., arXiv:2504.06972.]. Key signatures—including a fully spin and valley-polarized normal state, anomalous Hall resistivity hysteresis, superconducting phase adjacent to the fractional Chern ins…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266005] Published Tue Dec 30, 2025
item.dc:title: Chiral Superconductivity from Spin Polarized Chern Band in Twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Cheng Xu, Nianlong Zou, Nikolai Peshcherenko, Ammar Jahin, Tingxin Li, Shi-Zeng Lin, and Yang Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Superfluid Stiffness Bounds in Time-Reversal Symmetric Superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng and Andrew J. Millis

Quantum geometry has been shown to make an important contribution to the superfluid stiffness of flatband superconductors including moiré materials. In this Letter we use mean-field theory to derive an expression for the superfluid stiffness of time-reversal symmetric superconductors at zero tempera…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266004] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng and Andrew J. Millis

Quantum geometry has been shown to make an important contribution to the superfluid stiffness of flatband superconductors including moiré materials. In this Letter we use mean-field theory to derive an expression for the superfluid stiffness of time-reversal symmetric superconductors at zero tempera…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266004] Published Mon Dec 29, 2025
item.dc:title: Superfluid Stiffness Bounds in Time-Reversal Symmetric Superconductors
item.dc:creator: Yongxin Zeng and Andrew J. Millis
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Circular Dichroism on the Edge of Quantum Hall Systems: From Many-Body Chern Number to Anisotropy Measurements
item.description:
Author(s): F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman

Quantum Hall states are characterized by a topological invariant, the many-body Chern number, which determines their quantized Hall conductivity. This invariant also emerges in circular dichroic responses, namely, by applying a circular drive and comparing excitation rates for opposite orientations.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266603] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman

Quantum Hall states are characterized by a topological invariant, the many-body Chern number, which determines their quantized Hall conductivity. This invariant also emerges in circular dichroic responses, namely, by applying a circular drive and comparing excitation rates for opposite orientations.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266603] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: Circular Dichroism on the Edge of Quantum Hall Systems: From Many-Body Chern Number to Anisotropy Measurements
item.dc:creator: F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fast Hydrogen Atom Diffraction through Monocrystalline Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain

A fast beam of hydrogen atoms passes through a graphene layer and produces a diffraction pattern, a development that could lead to a new probing technique of surface interactions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 263403] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain

A fast beam of hydrogen atoms passes through a graphene layer and produces a diffraction pattern, a development that could lead to a new probing technique of surface interactions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 263403] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Fast Hydrogen Atom Diffraction through Monocrystalline Graphene
item.dc:creator: Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Spin-Depairing-Induced Exceptional Fermionic Superfluidity
item.description:
Author(s): Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga

We investigate the non-Hermitian (NH) attractive Hubbard model with spin depairing, which is a spin-resolved asymmetric hopping that nonreciprocally operates spins in the opposite direction. We find that spin depairing stabilizes a superfluid state unique to the NH system. This phase is characterize…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266002] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga

We investigate the non-Hermitian (NH) attractive Hubbard model with spin depairing, which is a spin-resolved asymmetric hopping that nonreciprocally operates spins in the opposite direction. We find that spin depairing stabilizes a superfluid state unique to the NH system. This phase is characterize…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266002] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Spin-Depairing-Induced Exceptional Fermionic Superfluidity
item.dc:creator: Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
